output.project_name: Project 1 Start project esting claude code
output.project_description: This project is about setting up the proper folder structure and index of all files that needs to be built for this application.  Each folder should have a small script file describing in 200 words what should go into the folder and in a root folder called /docs should be an index file listing the proposed folder architecture for the project.  You are also to prepare an initial readme.md no more than 500 words and requirments.txt

agent_templates.name: analyzer
agent_templates.description: Analysis specialist for requirements breakdown and technical planning

agent_templates.name: documenter
agent_templates.description: Documentation specialist for clear, comprehensive project documentation

agent_templates.name: tester
agent_templates.description: Testing specialist for comprehensive test coverage and quality assurance

agent_templates.name: implementer
agent_templates.description: Implementation specialist for writing production-grade code

agent_templates.name: reviewer
agent_templates.description: Code review specialist for quality assurance and best practices enforcement

agent_spawning_constraint.instruction: CRITICAL: You MUST use Claude Code's native Task tool for agent spawning. The agent_type parameter must be EXACTLY one of the allowed template names. Use agent_name for descriptive labels (displayed in UI). Allowed agent types: ['analyzer', 'documenter', 'tester', 'implementer', 'reviewer']

cli_mode_rules.agent_type_usage: MUST match template 'name' field exactly for Task tool. This is the filename without .md extension (e.g., 'implementer', 'analyzer').
cli_mode_rules.agent_name_usage: Descriptive label for UI display only - NOT for Task tool. Can be any human-readable name (e.g., 'Folder Structure Implementer').
cli_mode_rules.task_tool_mapping: Task(subagent_type=X) where X = agent_type value from spawn_agent_job. Claude Code's Task tool finds agents by filename, so agent_type must match exactly.

agent_type_is_truth.statement: agent_type is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for Task tool operations
agent_type_is_truth.agent_name_purpose: Display label ONLY - never for tool calling

spawning_examples.note: Both use agent_type='implementer' - the template name
